objective:
FICHE_STRATEGIE v1
id: momentum_macd
archetype: momentum_macd
family: momentum
timeframe: 1h
side: both
indicators:
- macd(fast_period=10, slow_period=24, signal_period=10)
- rsi(period=16)
- atr(period=14)
entry:
- long: cross_up(macd.macd, macd.signal) and rsi > 45 and rsi < 65
- short: cross_down(macd.macd, macd.signal) and rsi > 30 and rsi < 60
exit:
- condition: sign_change(macd.histogram) or rsi > 80 or rsi < 20
risk:
stop_atr_mult: 2.5
tp_atr_mult: 2.5
description: ATR stop-loss (2.5x) and take-profit (2.5x)
constraints:
- no_lookahead: true
- only_registry_indicators: true

iterations.hypothesis: Fallback contractuel: proposition générée automatiquement pour maintenir la progression quand la sortie LLM n'est pas exploitable.

pre_reflection.text:
[Pre-reflection] I've reviewed this strategy and identified its main components. I will critique it as follows:
1. Potential weaknesses are the dependence on multiple indicators, which may not always provide consistent signals.
2. If results are poor (negative return), I would consider adjusting stop-loss and take-profit parameters or testing different combinations of indicators.
3. For mediocre returns (low Sharpe ratio), I might try optimizing the parameters further or exploring other technical indicators to improve performance.
4. In case this strategy fails, an alternative approach could be a trend-following strategy using similar principles but relying on single or few well-performing indicators such as moving averages.

iterations.hypothesis: Le signal de Bollinger combiné à la DI (Direction Indicateur de Marche) réduit les faux signaux dans les marchés oscillants.

pre_reflection.text:
[Pre-reflection] Iteration 3/10 has been completed, no results are available yet. Possible weaknesses of the strategy include potential false signals in ranging markets and sensitivity to parameter choices that affect the performance of individual indicators. If Sharpe is poor (negative return), changing the stop-loss or take profit levels might be attempted first. If performance remains mediocre with low Sharpe, adjusting entry conditions by modifying RSI thresholds could be tried next.
[Backup plan] If necessary, alternative approaches may include using more sophisticated trend filters like moving averages and bollinger bands, incorporating multiple time frame analysis, testing other combinations of indicators or trading strategies (e.g., news sentiment, volume-based strategies), or further optimizing parameters for the existing strategy.
